subject: Physical Education (WASSCE)
document_title: West African Senior School Certificate Examination Physical Education Syllabus
objectives: Understanding of movement activities and the relationships between movement and concepts from biological, physical and social sciences
Skills needed in basic motor activities, fitness, maintenance and self awareness
An appreciation of the role of movement and physical activity in human development
The understanding of physical activity and exercise as tools for health and wellness promotion

sections: Section A: Athletics, balls and racket games (3 short essay questions, answer 2 for 20 marks)
Section B: Foundation of physical education (2 short essay questions, answer 1 for 10 marks)
Section C: Basic human anatomy and physiology in physical education (2 short essay questions, answer 1 for 10 marks)
Section D: Sports administration and competitions (2 short essay questions, answer 1 for 10 marks)
Section A: Athletics (Track and Field events) - 3 questions, answer 2 (25 marks each)
Section B: Ball games - 2 questions, answer 1 (20 marks)
Section C: Racket games - 2 questions, answer 1 (20 marks)
Section D: Gymnastics - 2 questions, answer 1 (10 marks)

detailed_syllabus.section_name: A. Principles and Philosophy of Physical Education
contents: Philosophy of Physical Education (Principles and philosophies of founding fathers like Hetherington, Dudley Sergent, Thomas Wood, and John Dewey; changes in concept; education of the physical vs. education through the physical)
Physical education ideologies (Nationalism, patriotism, and national ideologies in physical education and sport)
Recreation (Definition, importance, types of indoor/outdoor activities, differences between recreational activities and lifetime sports)
Intramural and Extramural activities And tournament (Definitions, benefits, types of tournaments, advantages/disadvantages)
Traditional sports in West Africa (Origin, types, and values in different West African countries)
Greek Festivals and The Olympic Games (Ancient Greek festivals/Olympian games, Sparta and Athens, origin and importance of modern Olympic Games)

detailed_syllabus.section_name: B. National and International Sports Competitions
contents: Sports Championship at national level (National championships by sports associations/federations, corporate organizations, and the national sports festival)
Institutional sports (Universities games, polytechnic games, colleges of education games, national school sports federation)
The All African Games (History, reasons for timing disparity, Supreme Council of Sports in Africa / SCSA)
International Competitions (FIFA World Cup, Olympic/Paralympic Games, Commonwealth Games, All Africa Games, World Athletics Championship, World Swimming Championship)

detailed_syllabus.section_name: C. Basic Human Anatomy and Physiology in Relation to Physical Activities
contents: Skeletal System (Main parts, functions of the human skeleton, bones and joints involved in movement)
Nervous System (Brain, spinal cord, nerves, simple reflex action)
Circulatory System (Heart muscle, effects of exercise on the heart, blood circulation, function of blood, types of circulation)
Respiratory System (Trachea, lungs, nose, functions, types of respiration, aerobic and anaerobic sports)
Skeletal muscles (Major muscles, types of muscle contraction, muscle cramp causes/prevention/first aid)
Somatotype (Description of body types, relationship between body type and physical activities/sports)
Posture (Correct posture, postural defects such as kyphosis, scoliosis, lordosis, flat foot, corrective measures)

detailed_syllabus.section_name: D. Practice of Athletics, Games and Dance
contents: Athletics - Track Events (Sprints 50m-400m, hurdles, relay races with visual/non-visual baton take-over, middle/long distance, marathon, rules, officiating)
Athletics - Field Events (Throwing events like discus, javelin, shot put; jumps like high jump, long jump, triple jump, pole vault; equipment, techniques, rules, safety)
Games - Ball Games (Football, basketball, volleyball, hockey, handball, rules, regulations, officiating, skills)
Racket Games (Tennis, table tennis, badminton, court specifications, equipment, skills, rules, officiating)
Traditional Dances (Dances from regional countries, costumes, types of traditional dance and steps)
Physical fitness (Definition, health-related and performance/skill-related components, benefits of physical activity and exercise)
Health and Personal hygiene (Importance of health in PE, care of the body, appropriate sportswear selection and effects)
Nutrition (Classes of food nutrients, balanced diet, dietary considerations in sports like pre-game nutrition and role of carbohydrates)
Drug in sports (Meaning, types of drugs such as stimulants, narcotics, hallucinogens, sedatives, ergogenic aids, misuse and effects on performance)
Sports injuries (Common injuries like dislocation, sprain, strain, fracture, bruise, cramp; first aid and first aid box contents)
Corrective and adapted physical education programme (Definitions, categories of special needs, corrective exercises, artificial respiration, safety precautions)
Administrative structure and functions of sports at national, regional/state, and local government levels (organograms)
Administrative structure and functions of sports at institutional level (organograms)
